description:
Hi team,

Linda Carlisle at Heartland Ag Exports here. We're shipping a container of soybeans to a buyer in Vietnam. Do we need an export license for this, or does it fall under a license exception? Want to confirm before the booking is finalized.

Thanks,
Linda
subject: Export license requirement for soybean shipment to Vietnam

description:
Hi team,

Rachel Combs at Heartland Ag Exports here. Our Mexican customer is asking for a USMCA certificate of origin on the corn shipment. Can you confirm the corn qualifies and send us the certificate template to complete?

Thanks,
Rachel
subject: USMCA certificate of origin for corn export to Mexico

description:
Hi team,

Gerald Maddox at Heartland Ag Exports here. The destination country requires a phytosanitary certificate for our wheat. Who issues it and how far ahead of the vessel do we need to request it so it doesn't hold the shipment?

Thanks,
Gerald
subject: Phytosanitary certificate for grain export

description:
Hi team,

Monica Frye at Heartland Ag Exports here. This shipment is over the $2,500 threshold per Schedule B number. Do we need to file the EEI in AES, and can your team handle that filing for us?

Thanks,
Monica
subject: EEI filing in AES for a high-value export

description:
Hi team,

Brian Holt at Heartland Ag Exports here. We've started exporting a processed oat product and aren't sure of the right Schedule B code. Can you confirm the classification so the AES filing is correct?

Thanks,
Brian
subject: Schedule B classification for processed oats

description:
Hi team,

Olivia Reyes at Heartland Ag Exports here. We have a new buyer in a country we haven't shipped to before. Can you run a denied-party and sanctions screening before we accept the order?

Thanks,
Olivia
subject: Denied party screening on a new buyer

description:
Hi team,

Nathan Boyle at Heartland Ag Exports here. Our buyer is paying by letter of credit and the bank has strict document requirements. Can you review our commercial invoice and packing list against the LC terms before we present?

Thanks,
Nathan
subject: Export documentation for a letter of credit shipment

description:
Hi team,

Diane Foster at Heartland Ag Exports here. Does our bagged seed product need country-of-origin marking for the export market, and is there a specific format the destination customs expects?

Thanks,
Diane
subject: Country of origin marking for bagged seed export

description:
Hi team,

Kevin Tran at Heartland Ag Exports here. We're exploring exporting to the EU. What duty rate would our customer face on our grain product, and is there any preferential treatment available?

Thanks,
Kevin
subject: Tariff treatment for our product in the EU market

description:
Hi team,

Samuel Ortega at Heartland Ag Exports here. How long do we need to retain export records like the EEI, bills of lading, and certificates? Want to set a retention policy for the export side.

Thanks,
Samuel
subject: Recordkeeping for export transactions

description:
Hi team,

Yolanda Pace at Heartland Ag Exports here. Canada is asking about our food facility registration. Do we need an FDA registration to export, and does it cover Canadian shipments?

Thanks,
Yolanda
subject: FDA registration for food export to Canada

description:
Hi team,

Roland Beck at Heartland Ag Exports here. Buyer wants CIF terms but we usually sell FOB. What changes for us on documentation and risk if we switch to CIF on this shipment?

Thanks,
Roland
subject: Incoterms question for a new ocean shipment

description:
Hi team,

Theresa Lambert at Heartland Ag Exports here. We're exporting a fertilizer product that may be classified as hazardous for ocean transport. What documentation does the carrier need and can you help prepare it?

Thanks,
Theresa
subject: Hazmat documentation for a fertilizer export

description:
Hi team,

Marcus Reed at Heartland Ag Exports here. We imported packaging materials with duty, then used them on goods we exported. Can we claim drawback on that duty, and what records do we keep?

Thanks,
Marcus
subject: Drawback on duty paid for re-exported packaging

description:
Hi team,

Paula Reinhart at Heartland Ag Exports here. One of our exports includes an ag-tech sensor component. Can you confirm whether it has an ECCN or is EAR99 before we ship internationally?

Thanks,
Paula
subject: ECCN classification check for an ag-tech component

description:
Hi team,

Grant Holloway at Heartland Ag Exports here. The importing country requires a certificate of free sale for our processed food. Who issues that and can you walk us through getting one?

Thanks,
Grant
subject: Certificate of free sale for a food export

description:
Hi team,

Renee Vandenberg at Heartland Ag Exports here. We found an error in the quantity on an EEI after the vessel left. How do we file the correction in AES and is there any penalty exposure?

Thanks,
Renee
subject: AES filing correction after vessel departure

description:
Hi team,

Henry Castillo at Heartland Ag Exports here. Our foreign buyer wants to control the freight, making this a routed export transaction. What are our responsibilities versus the buyer's forwarder for the EEI?

Thanks,
Henry
subject: Routed export transaction responsibilities

description:
Hi team,

Vera Sandoval at Heartland Ag Exports here. We're sending demo equipment abroad for a trade show and bringing it back. Is there a carnet or temporary-export process that avoids paying duty on return?

Thanks,
Vera
subject: Temporary export of equipment for a trade show

description:
Hi team,

Curtis Bauer at Heartland Ag Exports here. We'd like your firm to review our export compliance procedures end to end and flag any gaps. Can we set this up as a one-time engagement?

Thanks,
Curtis
subject: Export compliance program review

description:
Hi team,

Linda Carlisle at Heartland Ag Exports here. Our shipment routes through a port in a country under partial sanctions before reaching the buyer. Does the transshipment create any compliance issue we need to address?

Thanks,
Linda
subject: Sanctions question on a transshipment route

description:
Hi team,

Rachel Combs at Heartland Ag Exports here. We reformulated one of our exported food products. Does the Schedule B number change, and do we need to update our AES profile?

Thanks,
Rachel
subject: Schedule B update for a reformulated product
